intelligence.userFlowMarkdown:

2. **Open project** — folder binds workspace; Orca scans stack + architecture
3. **Canvas** — spatial tiles: terminals, browser, plan, code, performance
4. **Command** — voice or text to orchestrator; triage → decompose or direct tools
5. **Agents** — parallel workers on canvas; lead synthesizes handoffs

planExcerpt:
# Plan

**Status:** Advising — Advisory via Hermes

## Request
hi

## Understanding
Hermes is reviewing your project and drafting next steps.

## Build Plan
### Next steps (from advisory)

- [ ] Hey — welcome back. Three items waiting from the NativeVibe integration: delete the stale PLAN.md, commit everything, and push. Say the word and I'll walk the canvas through it. What's on your mind?

## Tech Stack
**Languages:** Swift, PNG, MD, SH
**Platforms:** macOS

**Hierarchy** (presentation → core):
1. **Shell & View** — SwiftUI, AppKit, target:OrcaCoder, target:OrcaCLI · _OrcaCoderSettingsView, OrcaCoderApp, CanvasWelcomeView, CanvasChromeInsets_
2. **Canvas System** — SwiftUI, Spatial layout · _CanvasPanMomentum, LoFiWallpaperStore, CanvasLayoutPresets, CanvasPlanTileView_
3. **Orchestrator** — LLM tool loop, Command dispatch · _OrchestratorLog, CommandDispatcher, BridgeOrchestratorPipeline, DecompositionActivity_
4. **Agent Runtime** — PTY, CLI spawn, SwiftTerm · _AgentCLI, SubAgentConcurrencyGate, AgentDelegation, PTYSession_
5. **Utilities** — Persistence, Git, File I/O · _CLIClient, main, FreeformCanvasLayout, LocalProxyController_

**External dependencies:** SwiftTerm

## Dependencies
- **Build:** `swift build`
- **Test:** `swift test`
- **Package:** Swift PM (`Package.swift`)
- **Targets:** OrcaCoder, OrcaCLI
- **Platforms:** macOS
- **Web subtree:** `web/` for landing/marketing
- **VCS:** Git
- **Scripts:** `scripts/` test & automation harness

## User Flow
1. **Launch** — welcome screen or resume last canvas
2. **Open project** — folder binds workspace; Orca scans stack + architecture
3. **Canvas** — spatial tiles: terminals, browser, plan, code, performance
4. **Command** — voice or text to orchestrator; triage → decompose or direct tools
5. **Agents** — parallel workers on canvas; lead synthesizes handoffs
6. **Ship** — git commit, preview in browser tile, iterate

## Design Guide
_In progress…_

## Architecture
```mermaid
flowchart TD
  User["User Request"] --> Lead["Orca"]
  Lead --> Work["Orchestrating…"]
```
readmeExcerpt:
# Pip 🐾

A cute, transparent desktop mascot for macOS that **walks along the bottom edge of your
screen** with a real walk cycle, and changes mood based on your **live Claude Code
usage** — pulled from your logged-in Claude session. No API key, no cloud account, no
billing, no third-party dependencies. Everything stays on your machine.

Pip's goal is to help you **maximize your 5-hour window without wasting quota at reset**.
He doesn't panic as the reset approaches — he gets antsy when you're *under*-using your
quota, and only worries when you're about to hit the cap.

---

## ⚡️ One-shot setup (recommended)

**Copy the block below and paste it into [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code).**
It will clone, build, install, and launch Pip for you — and fix anything that goes wrong
along the way.

> ```
> Set up Pip, a macOS desktop mascot, on my Mac from
> https://github.com/TenzinDhonyoe/pip-mascot
>
> Do all of this for me, and troubleshoot anything that fails:
> 1. Clone the repo into ~/Developer (create it if needed) and cd into it.
> 2. Confirm I have full Xcode (not just the command-line tools). If `xcodebuild
>    -version` fails or points at CommandLineTools, tell me to 
…(truncated)
